task_completion.description: Verify that the OpenICE Supervisor application is running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenICE Supervisor graphical user interface (GUI) or its corresponding terminal process is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that a 'Multiparameter Monitor' device is being simulated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenICE GUI shows a 'Multiparameter Monitor' in the list of active or simulated devices.

task_completion.description: Verify that an 'Infusion Pump' device is being simulated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenICE GUI shows an 'Infusion Pump' in the list of active or simulated devices.

task_completion.description: Verify that the deployment audit text file has been created at the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows a file open or created at exactly '/home/ga/Desktop/deployment_audit.txt'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the OpenICE Java process JVM system properties are documented in the audit file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file '/home/ga/Desktop/deployment_audit.txt' contains JVM system properties, specifically including 'java.vm.name', 'java.version', and 'os.name' along with their corresponding values.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Active Devices:' section is included at the end of the audit file with the correct devices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The end of the text file contains the exact label 'Active Devices:' followed by a list containing 'Multiparameter Monitor' and 'Infusion Pump'.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent actually interacted with the OpenICE application to start the simulators.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the OpenICE GUI or using valid terminal commands to launch the specific device simulators, rather than just typing their names into the text file.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent extracted the JVM properties from the actual running process.
integrity.visual_evidence: A terminal window should show the agent using a diagnostic tool like 'jcmd <PID> VM.system_properties' or 'jinfo' to fetch the properties from the OpenICE Java process, rather than hallucinating or hardcoding standard values.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not simply copy-paste a pre-formatted answer without performing the audit.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values for the JVM properties (like the specific Java version or OS name) should match the actual system environment (e.g., OpenJDK 17, Linux) and appear to be copied from the terminal output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file must be located at '/home/ga/Desktop/deployment_audit.txt'. The required simulated devices to be listed are 'Multiparameter Monitor' and 'Infusion Pump'. The required JVM properties to document are 'java.vm.name', 'java.version', and 'os.name'. These properties are standard Java system properties typically returned by the 'jcmd <PID> VM.system_properties' command.
